spec_match.rationale: The demo now uses a real streamed shell plus later <template for> patches instead of a local ReadableStream timing simulation.

interactive.rationale: The page reloads the live route and logs streamed chunks, but native patch application still needs chrome-devtools-mcp verification.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb now describes minimal declarative parser patching.

edge_cases.rationale: The streamed document includes two independent slots and fallback detection for unsupported browsers.

references.rationale: The page links to the correct ChromeStatus entry and WICG explainer.

design_system.rationale: The page uses the shared live-patching styles and design tokens.

openQuestions.title: Verify native parser patching in Chrome 150
openQuestions.detail: Use chrome-devtools-mcp to load the live iframe and confirm both processing-instruction ranges are replaced by streamed template content.

summary: Replaced the generic stream simulation with a minimal server-backed declarative patch stream.
